sourceMovie.title: Slither
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Slither (2006) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Night of the Creeps
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Slither clicked for you, Night of the Creeps (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Slither?
faq.answer: Try Night of the Creeps, Cold Storage, Resident Evil—each shares DNA with Slither while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Slither?
faq.answer: Slither (2006) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Slither worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
